solution_summary: Fix the flight search functionality to ensure the 'max price' filter returns flights with prices that are less than or equal to the specified maximum price.
implementation_steps: Identify the current logic in the search_flights function in flight_service.py that processes the max_price filter.
Modify this logic to ensure that flights priced at or below the max_price are returned instead of flights priced above it.
Add or update unit tests to validate the correct behavior of the search functionality with respect to the max_price filter.

planned_changes.change_summary: Update the search_flights function to return flights whose prices are less than or equal to max_price.
planned_changes.rationale: To correct the error in the max price filter logic that currently returns flights over the specified price limit.

planned_changes.change_summary: Add or modify unit tests for the search_flights function to ensure the max price filter behaves as expected.
planned_changes.rationale: To validate that the corrected logic in the search_flights function works as intended.

patch_strategy: Create a new branch 'feature/SDLC-7-flight-search-max-price-filter-returns-the-most-expensive-flights' from the main branch. Apply the modifications to flight_service.py and unit tests as planned. Commit the changes with an appropriate message. Push the branch to the remote repository and create a pull request.
validation_steps: Run existing unit tests to ensure no other functionality is broken.
Add new unit tests for the max price filter functionality.
Run all tests to ensure they pass and validate the expected behavior.
pr_title: Fix max price filter in flight search functionality
pr_body: This PR addresses the issue described in SDLC-7 where the max price filter in the flight search functionality incorrectly returns flights that exceed the specified maximum price. The logic in the search_flights function has been updated to ensure it only returns flights priced at or below the given max price. Additionally, unit tests have been updated/added to validate this behavior.
risks: Potentially breaking existing functionality if the logic changes conflict with other filters in the search_flights function.
Insufficient test coverage could lead to undiscovered bugs if other related functionality is affected.
assumptions: The existing unit tests are comprehensive enough to catch any unintended effects of the changes made.
